title: What Is a Shidduch? Understanding Jewish Matchmaking
title_zh: 什么是配婚？理解犹太人的婚姻介绍
author: Torah Light Project

chinese_parallels.connection: Traditional Chinese marriage arrangements through intermediaries parallel the shadchan system
Chinese emphasis on family compatibility mirrors Jewish focus on shared values in shidduchim
Chinese concept of predestined matrimonial fate parallels Jewish belief in bashert

torah_sources.topic: Abraham's servant finding Rebecca for Isaac - the first shidduch
How marriage is acquired in Jewish law
Forty days before formation, a voice declares whose daughter this one will marry
Making matches is as difficult as splitting the Red Sea

key_concepts: Shidduch - Jewish approach to finding a marriage partner through community involvement
Bashert (בשרת) - divinely intended soulmate or life partner
Dating for the purpose of marriage, not casual relationship
Community and family involvement in the process
Balancing practical considerations with spiritual connection
Prayer and divine guidance in finding one's match
target_audience: Young Jewish adults beginning to think about marriage; families supporting children in shidduchim; Jewish communities in Chinese-speaking regions
practical_applications: Understanding the purpose and process of Jewish matchmaking
Preparing mentally and spiritually for shidduch dating
Setting appropriate expectations for the process
Appreciating community involvement in finding a life partner
Balancing personal preferences with family input
Developing patience and trust in divine timing
notes: Foundational article explaining the Jewish approach to marriage introduction, emphasizing community involvement, spiritual dimension, and purposeful dating. Includes Chinese cultural parallels on matchmaking and family harmony.
